title: The London System
summary: The universal system — same setup, every time

steps.title: Why Play London?
steps.text: The London System is a "universal" opening — White plays the same setup regardless of Black's response. Great for beginners: less theory, more reliable patterns.

steps.hint: Bishop goes BEFORE e3, so it's not blocked by its own pawns.

steps.title: The Full Setup
steps.text: The full London recipe: d4, Bf4, Nf3, e3, c3, Nbd2, Bd3, 0-0. Solid, symmetric-ish, hard to break down. Magnus Carlsen has used it in world championship matches.
